title: How to back up photos to Google Photos or iCloud
description: Ensure your precious memories are safely backed up in the cloud so you never lose them if your phone breaks.

steps.title: Connect to your home Wi-Fi
steps.body: Uploading hundreds of high-quality photos can take a lot of internet data. Make sure your phone is connected to your home Wi-Fi network and plugged into a charger.

steps.title: Enable iCloud Photo Backup on iPhone
steps.body: Open the 'Settings' app, tap your name at the very top of the list, tap 'iCloud', tap 'Photos', and toggle the switch for 'Sync this iPhone' to green.

steps.title: Enable Google Photos Backup on Android
steps.body: Open the Google Photos app (looks like a colorful pinwheel), tap your profile picture in the top-right corner, tap 'Photos settings', select 'Backup', and turn on the 'Backup' toggle switch.

steps.title: Verify your upload progress
steps.body: Keep the app open for a few minutes. You will see a small circular arrow icon spin, showing that photos are copying to the cloud. A checkmark means everything is backed up!

glossary.definition: Apple's cloud storage service that automatically keeps your iPhone photos, notes, and documents backed up safely online.
An app by Google that stores and organizes your photos in your online Google Account.
